title: पंचायतन पूजा स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री पंचायतन पूजा स्तोत्रम् ॥

पञ्चदेवपरं स्तोत्रं सर्वदेवनमस्कृतम्।
पञ्चायतनपूजार्थं पठेद्भक्त्या विचक्षणः॥

॥ गणपति स्तुति ॥
वक्रतुण्ड महाकाय सूर्यकोटिसमप्रभ।
निर्विघ्नं कुरु मे देव सर्वकार्येषु सर्वदा॥१॥

ॐ गं गणपतये नमः।

॥ शिव स्तुति ॥
वन्दे शम्भुमुमापतिं सुरगुरुं वन्दे जगत्कारणं
वन्दे पन्नगभूषणं मृगधरं वन्दे पशूनां पतिम्।
वन्दे सूर्यशशाङ्कवह्निनयनं वन्दे मुकुन्दप्रियं
वन्दे भक्तजनाश्रयं च वरदं वन्दे शिवं शङ्करम्॥२॥

ॐ नमः शिवाय।

॥ विष्णु स्तुति ॥
शान्ताकारं भुजगशयनं पद्मनाभं सुरेशं
विश्वाधारं गगनसदृशं मेघवर्णं शुभाङ्गम्।
लक्ष्मीकान्तं कमलनयनं योगिभिर्ध्यानगम्यं
वन्दे विष्णुं भवभयहरं सर्वलोकैकनाथम्॥३॥

ॐ नमो नारायणाय।

॥ सूर्य स्तुति ॥
जपाकुसुमसङ्काशं काश्यपेयं महद्द्युतिम्।
तमोऽरिं सर्वपापघ्नं प्रणतोऽस्मि दिवाकरम्॥४॥

ॐ आदित्याय नमः।

॥ देवी स्तुति ॥
सर्वमङ्गलमाङ्गल्ये शिवे सर्वार्थसाधिके।
शरण्ये त्र्यम्बके गौरि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥५॥

ॐ दुर्गायै नमः।

॥ एकत्र प्रार्थना ॥
गणेशं शङ्करं विष्णुं सूर्यं देवीं च पञ्चमीम्।
पञ्चायतनमित्याहुः पूजयेद्यो विमुक्तये॥६॥

ब्रह्मण्यं सर्वलोकेशं पञ्चदेवात्मकं परम्।
पञ्चायतनपूजायां सर्वदेवाः प्रसीदन्तु॥७॥

आदिशङ्कराचार्येण प्रवर्तितमिदं महत्।
स्मार्तधर्मस्य मूलं हि पञ्चायतनपूजनम्॥८॥

यस्य देवे परा भक्तिर्यथा देवे तथा गुरौ।
पञ्चदेवसमभ्यर्च्य मोक्षमार्गं व्रजेन्नरः॥९॥

गणपतिः शिवो विष्णुः सूर्यो देवी च पञ्चमी।
एतेषां पूजनादेव सर्वसिद्धिरवाप्यते॥१०॥

ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥

॥ इति श्री पंचायतन पूजा स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Pañchāyatana Pūjā Stotram ||

Pañchadevaparaṁ stotraṁ sarvadevanamaskṛtam.
Pañchāyatanapūjārthaṁ paṭhedbhaktyā vichakṣaṇaḥ.

|| Gaṇapati Stuti ||
Vakratuṇḍa Mahākāya Sūryakoṭisamaprabha.
Nirvighnaṁ kuru me deva sarvakāryeṣu sarvadā.||1||

Om Gaṁ Gaṇapataye Namaḥ.

|| Śiva Stuti ||
Vande Śambhumamāpatiṁ suraguruṁ vande jagatkāraṇaṁ
Vande pannagabhūṣaṇaṁ mṛgadharaṁ vande paśūnāṁ patim.
Vande sūryaśaśāṅkavahninayanaṁ vande Mukundapriyaṁ
Vande bhaktajanāśrayaṁ cha varadaṁ vande Śivaṁ Śaṅkaram.||2||

Om Namaḥ Śivāya.

|| Viṣṇu Stuti ||
Śāntākāraṁ bhujagaśayanaṁ Padmanābhaṁ sureśaṁ
Viśvādhāraṁ gaganasadṛśaṁ meghavarṇaṁ śubhāṅgam.
Lakṣmīkāntaṁ kamalanayanaṁ yogibhirdhyānagamyaṁ
Vande Viṣṇuṁ bhavabhayaharaṁ sarvalokaikanātham.||3||

Om Namo Nārāyaṇāya.

|| Sūrya Stuti ||
Japākusumasaṅkāśaṁ Kāśyapeyaṁ mahaddyutim.
Tamo'riṁ sarvapāpaghnaṁ praṇato'smi Divākaram.||4||

Om Ādityāya Namaḥ.

|| Devī Stuti ||
Sarvamaṅgalamāṅgalye Śive sarvārthasādhike.
Śaraṇye Tryambake Gauri Nārāyaṇi namo'stu te.||5||

Om Durgāyai Namaḥ.

|| Ekatra Prārthanā ||
Gaṇeśaṁ Śaṅkaraṁ Viṣṇuṁ Sūryaṁ Devīṁ cha pañchamīm.
Pañchāyatanamityāhuḥ pūjayedyo vimuktaye.||6||

Brahmaṇyaṁ sarvalokeśaṁ pañchadevātmakaṁ param.
Pañchāyatanapūjāyāṁ sarvadevāḥ prasīdantu.||7||

ĀdiŚaṅkarāchāryeṇa pravartitamidaṁ mahat.
Smārtadharmasya mūlaṁ hi pañchāyatanapūjanam.||8||

Yasya deve parā bhaktiryathā deve tathā gurau.
Pañchadevasamabhyarchya mokṣamārgaṁ vrajennaaraḥ.||9||

Gaṇapatiḥ Śivo Viṣṇuḥ Sūryo Devī cha pañchamī.
Eteṣāṁ pūjanādeva sarvasiddhiravāpyate.||10||

Om Śāntiḥ Śāntiḥ Śāntiḥ.

|| Iti Shrī Pañchāyatana Pūjā Stotraṁ sampūrṇam ||
hindiMeaning: पाँच देवों का यह स्तोत्र सर्वदेवों द्वारा नमस्कृत है, पञ्चायतन पूजा के लिए बुद्धिमान भक्ति से पढ़ें। (1) गणपति: वक्रतुण्ड, महाकाय, करोड़ सूर्य जैसी प्रभा - सर्व कार्यों में निर्विघ्नता दें। (2) शिव: उमापति, जगत के कारण, सर्प भूषण, पशुपति, त्रिनेत्र, मुकुन्दप्रिय, भक्तों के आश्रय शिव शंकर को वन्दन। (3) विष्णु: शान्ताकार, भुजंगशयन, पद्मनाभ, विश्वाधार, मेघवर्ण, लक्ष्मीकान्त, कमलनयन, भवभयहर, सर्वलोक के एकमात्र नाथ विष्णु को वन्दन। (4) सूर्य: गुड़हल पुष्प जैसे, महातेजस्वी, सर्वपापघ्न दिवाकर को प्रणाम। (5) देवी: सर्वमंगलमांगल्ये, सर्वार्थसाधिके, शरण्ये, त्र्यम्बके, गौरी, नारायणी को नमस्कार। (6-8) गणेश, शंकर, विष्णु, सूर्य और देवी - ये पञ्चायतन हैं, मुक्ति के लिए इनकी पूजा करे। आदिशंकराचार्य ने यह स्मार्त धर्म का मूल स्थापित किया। (10) इन पाँचों की पूजा से ही सर्वसिद्धि प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Combines worship of all five principal deities in the Smarta tradition
Established by Adi Shankaracharya as the foundation of Smarta Dharma
Contains the most beloved stutis - Vakratunda, Shantakaram, Sarva Mangala
Worshipping all five deities grants complete spiritual accomplishment
Leads to Moksha (liberation) through balanced five-deity devotion
Each deity receives their respective mula mantra for proper invocation
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Vishnu, Panchayatana Puja Stotram (पंचायतन पूजा स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Attributed to Smarta Tradition / Adi Shankaracharya, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to established by Adi Shankaracharya as the foundation of Smarta Dharma as well as lead to Moksha (liberation) through balanced five-deity devotion. Additionally, it is known to combine worship of all five principal deities in the Smarta tradition. For best results, devotees chant this during Navratri. With 10 verses and a reading time of about 7 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Panchayatana Puja Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Panchayatana Puja Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Five-deity worship established by Adi Shankaracharya for complete spiritual growth.
relatedSearches: Panchayatana puja vidhi step by step
Pancha Devata names Vishnu Shiva Devi Surya Ganesha
Adi Shankaracharya Smarta tradition five deities
Panchayatana puja shaligram bana linga arrangement
Pancha Devata symbols and stones for puja
Panchayatana puja for daily Brahmin household

seoFaqs.question: Why is Adi Shankara credited with formalising Panchayatana Puja?
seoFaqs.answer: By the eighth century the Indian devotional landscape had fragmented into competing sectarian schools of Vaishnavas, Shaivas, Shaktas, Sauras and Ganapatyas. Adi Shankara, who taught the non-dual Advaita Vedanta in which all named deities point to one undivided Brahman, codified the Panchayatana arrangement so that adherents of any of the five streams could continue worshipping their ishta-devata while equally honouring the other four. The system therefore performed both a theological and a social function, harmonising sectarian rivalries within a single domestic puja. The arrangement became the standard daily practice of Smarta Brahmin households, especially across South India and the Konkan. (Sources: Wikipedia Panchayatana Puja; DharmaRenaissance Blog.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the quincunx arrangement of the five deities?
seoFaqs.answer: Panchayatana Puja arranges the five murtis or aniconic stones in a quincunx, that is one at the centre with four at the corners. The devotee's chosen ishta-devata occupies the centre and the other four are placed at the cardinal corners. A Vaishnava-centric arrangement places Vishnu (a shaligram) at the centre with Shiva, Devi, Surya and Ganesha around it; a Shaiva-centric arrangement substitutes a bana-linga at the centre, and so on for Shakta, Saura and Ganapatya households. This rotational scheme is what allows the same five deities to anchor every sectarian preference within one orthodox framework. (Sources: Wikipedia Panchayatana Puja; HinduPad.)

seoFaqs.question: Which natural symbols traditionally represent each of the five deities?
seoFaqs.answer: Smarta tradition uses aniconic stones drawn from specific sacred sites: Vishnu is represented by a shaligram from the Gandaki river in Nepal, Shiva by a bana-linga from the Narmada, Devi by a swarna-rekha quartz or a red stone from Kanyakumari, Surya by a sphatika (rock crystal) or a stone from the Vallam region, and Ganesha by a red sonabhadra stone from the Sone river. These five together form the Pancha-shila or Pancha-ayatana set, often sold pre-bundled by traditional puja suppliers. The aniconic format reinforces the Advaita teaching that Brahman transcends all images. (Sources: Wikipedia Panchayatana Puja; Vedic Vaani.)

seoFaqs.question: Does Panchayatana Puja require five separate stotrams or a single composite hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: The standard daily liturgy uses one short composite invocation, typically titled Pancha Devata Puja Stotram or Panchayatana Pujana Mantra, that names and salutes the five deities in a fixed order. Following this invocation, devotees may recite the deity-specific stotrams of their lineage: Vishnu Sahasranama or Bhagavad-Gita chapter 15 for Vishnu, Rudram or Shiva Panchakshara for Shiva, Devi Mahatmya for Devi, Aditya Hridayam for Surya and Ganapati Atharvashirsha for Ganesha. The composite invocation is therefore the entry point, with longer hymns added based on time available and family custom. (Sources: animedevta.com; HinduPad.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Panchayatana relate to Shanmata, the six-school tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Shanmata, also attributed to Adi Shankara, expands the Panchayatana five into six by adding Skanda (Kartikeya, the Kaumara stream) to Vishnu, Shiva, Devi, Surya and Ganesha. Panchayatana is the five-deity domestic puja arrangement; Shanmata is the broader doctrinal recognition of six legitimate sectarian paths within the Smarta fold. In Tamil Nadu and parts of the Deccan where Murugan worship is dominant, the six-deity Shanmata model is more visible, while in northern Smarta households the five-deity Panchayatana set remains the normative daily framework. (Sources: Vedadhara Shanmatha; DharmaRenaissance Blog.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the historical evidence for Panchayatana before Adi Shankara?
seoFaqs.answer: Although tradition credits Adi Shankara as the systematiser, archaeological evidence shows the five-deity grouping pre-dates him by several centuries. Sculptural Panchayatana mandalas from the Gupta period (4th to 6th century CE) survive at Devgarh, Nachna and other sites, and a Panchayatana set excavated from the village of Nand near Ajmer dates to the Kushana era, before 300 CE. Shankara's contribution was to standardise the puja procedure and assign each sectarian school its rotation rather than to invent the grouping itself. The continuity from Gupta sculpture to modern Smarta puja represents one of the longest unbroken iconographic traditions in Hinduism. (Sources: Wikipedia Panchayatana Puja; Indianetzone.)
